name: quality-manager-qmr
description: Senior Quality Manager Responsible Person (QMR) for HealthTech and MedTech companies. Provides overall quality system responsibility, regulatory compliance oversight, management accountability, and strategic quality leadership. Use for quality system governance, regulatory compliance oversight, management responsibility, and quality strategic planning.
capabilities: Provides overall quality management system oversight and accountability
Guides regulatory compliance across EU MDR, FDA QSR, and ISO 13485 requirements
Leads management review processes with strategic quality planning
Develops quality KPIs and performance monitoring frameworks
Establishes quality culture and organizational quality leadership
Manages regulatory authority relationships and certification programs
author: alirezarezvani
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown files with quality management frameworks. No executable code, scripts, network operations, or cryptographic operations exist. All 63 static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misidentifying ASCII tree diagram characters as shell backticks, file format names as cryptographic patterns, and regulatory compliance text as security threats.
valueStatement: HealthTech and MedTech companies face complex quality system requirements across multiple jurisdictions. This skill provides senior quality manager expertise for ISO 13485 compliance, regulatory oversight, and strategic quality leadership.
userTitle: Lead Quality Management for HealthTech Organizations
limitations: Does not execute regulatory submissions or interact with authorities directly
Does not replace qualified legal or regulatory affairs professionals
Does not provide device-specific clinical or technical documentation

useCases.title: Strategic Quality Planning
useCases.description: Develop quality strategies aligned with business objectives and regulatory requirements across multiple markets.

useCases.title: Compliance Oversight
useCases.description: Monitor multi-jurisdictional compliance status and prepare for regulatory authority inspections and audits.

useCases.title: QMS Implementation
useCases.description: Establish quality management system governance, document controls, and continuous improvement processes.

promptTemplates.title: QMS Overview
promptTemplates.prompt: Provide an overview of QMR responsibilities for a medical device company seeking ISO 13485 certification.

promptTemplates.title: Compliance Assessment
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the key compliance requirements for FDA QSR 21 CFR 820 and EU MDR 2017/745 for a Class II medical device?

promptTemplates.title: Management Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create a quarterly management review agenda covering quality system effectiveness, regulatory compliance, and quality culture.

promptTemplates.title: Quality Strategy
promptTemplates.prompt: Develop a 3-year quality strategic plan for a growing MedTech company expanding from US to European markets.

bestPractices: Integrate quality considerations into product development from concept through commercialization
Maintain proactive regulatory intelligence to anticipate compliance requirement changes
Establish clear quality metrics and review them regularly with senior leadership
antiPatterns: Waiting until audit findings to address systemic quality issues
Treating quality management as a documentation exercise rather than a cultural commitment
Neglecting to involve cross-functional teams in quality system governance

faq.question: What is the difference between QMR and a Quality Assurance Manager?
faq.answer: QMR has organizational accountability for quality system effectiveness, while QA managers handle day-to-day quality operations.

faq.question: How often should management reviews be conducted?
faq.answer: ISO 13485 requires documented management review at planned intervals. Quarterly reviews are industry standard.

faq.question: What qualifications does a QMR need?
faq.answer: Regulatory requirements vary, but typically include quality system experience, regulatory knowledge, and organizational authority.

faq.question: How do EU MDR and FDA QSR requirements differ?
faq.answer: EU MDR emphasizes clinical evaluation and post-market surveillance, while FDA QSR focuses on design controls and manufacturing.

faq.question: Can this skill help with ISO 13485 certification preparation?
faq.answer: Yes, it provides guidance on certification requirements, audit preparation, and continuous improvement for maintaining certification.

faq.question: What quality metrics should we track?
faq.answer: Key metrics include process effectiveness, customer satisfaction, audit findings, CAPA closure rates, and product quality indicators.
